danish_text_clean:
Facebook-stifteren Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser: En kunstigt intelligent klon af ham selv.

Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format og kan derefter holde møder med medarbejderne i Facebook-moderselskabet, Meta.

USA's teknologigiganter sprøjter milliarder ind i et ai-eventyr, som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af. Natten til torsdag kom der nye vilde tal, fortæller Michael Jarlner om i denne kommentar.
english_translation:
Facebook founder Mark Zuckerberg is in the process of finding a replacement: An artificially intelligent clone of himself.

The clone will, according to plan, appear in an advanced 3D format and can then hold meetings with employees at Facebook's parent company, Meta.

The US technology giants are pumping billions into an AI adventure that no one can fully grasp the scope or consequences of. On Thursday morning, new wild figures emerged, which Michael Jarlner discusses in this commentary.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Er Zuckerbergs nyeste påfund alligevel for vildt?
vocabulary.note: Carries a slightly ironic or skeptical tone — implies the idea is eccentric or outlandish.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Facebook-stifteren Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser.
vocabulary.note: Definite form of 'stifter'. Commonly appears in compounds: 'stifter' alone or 'virksomhedsstifter' (company founder).

vocabulary.example_from_article: Mark Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser.
vocabulary.note: From the verb 'at afløse' (to relieve, replace). Also used in sports for a substitute player.

vocabulary.example_from_article: En kunstigt intelligent klon af ham selv.
vocabulary.note: Used here as an adverb modifying 'intelligent'. 'Kunstig intelligens' = artificial intelligence. The -t ending appears when 'kunstig' is used adverbially.

vocabulary.english: to appear, to perform, to act
vocabulary.example_from_article: Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format.
vocabulary.note: Can mean both 'to perform on stage' and 'to appear/show up'. Context determines which meaning applies.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Klonen vil efter planen optræde i et avanceret 3D-format.
vocabulary.note: Borrowed from French via English. Widely used in Danish for technology, skills, and ideas.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Holde møder med medarbejderne i Facebook-moderselskabet, Meta.
vocabulary.note: Compound: 'moder' (mother) + 'selskab' (company). Danish uses 'moder-' in business compounds where English says 'parent'.

vocabulary.english: to pump, to inject, to spray
vocabulary.example_from_article: USA's teknologigiganter sprøjter milliarder ind i et ai-eventyr.
vocabulary.note: Literally 'to spray/squirt'. Used figuratively here with 'ind i' (into) to mean pouring money into something — vivid journalistic language.

vocabulary.english: to grasp fully, to comprehend, to get an overview of
vocabulary.example_from_article: Som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af.
vocabulary.note: False-friend risk: does NOT mean 'to overlook' (ignore). 'Jeg kan ikke overskue det' = I can't get my head around it / it's overwhelming.

vocabulary.example_from_article: Som ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af.
vocabulary.note: Frequent in formal and journalistic Danish. 'I stort omfang' = to a large extent.

grammar_notes.topic: Modal verb 'kan' (can / to be able to)
grammar_notes.explanation: 'Kan derefter holde møder' and 'ingen kan overskue' both use 'kan', present tense of 'at kunne'. Like all Danish modals, it is followed by a bare infinitive — no 'at'. 'Kan' expresses ability or possibility and is one of the most frequent modal verbs in Danish.

'Klonen kan derefter holde møder' — 'derefter' indicates temporal sequence: something happens first, then this follows. It belongs to a formal/written register. In spoken Danish, 'så' (so/then) or 'bagefter' (afterwards) are more common equivalents.

'Ingen kan overskue hverken omfanget eller konsekvensen af' uses 'hverken…eller' to negate two items in parallel, equivalent to English 'neither…nor'. The negation is already carried by 'ingen' (no one), so 'hverken…eller' adds emphatic parallel structure. Learn these correlative pairs together: 'både…og' (both…and), 'enten…eller' (either…or), 'hverken…eller' (neither…nor).

grammar_notes.topic: Progressive construction 'er i gang med at + infinitive'
grammar_notes.explanation: 'Zuckerberg er i gang med at finde en afløser' — Danish has no true present progressive (-ing form). The phrase 'er i gang med at' (literally: is in the process of) followed by an infinitive fills this gap and emphasises that the action is actively ongoing. It is more emphatic than the simple present 'finder'.

discussion_questions: Hvad synes du om ideen om at bruge en AI-klon til at holde møder på arbejde?
Hvilke problemer kan der opstå, hvis en AI-klon repræsenterer en rigtig person?
Er det godt eller dårligt, at store teknologivirksomheder investerer så mange penge i kunstig intelligens?
title: Er Zuckerbergs nyeste påfund alligevel for vildt?
